traits.quirk: has an enemy (Berrian, a elf mercenary captain, level 3, out there somewhere)

q01.name: The Dragon's Tithe
q01.desc: A whole valley pays it tribute and calls that peace. The crown calls it rebellion with wings.
q01.epilogue: The valley keeps its harvest for the first year in living memory; the tithe-barns are rebuilt as granaries.

q02.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q02.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q02.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q03.name: The Blighted Glade
q03.desc: Something soured the heartwood; what lives there now bites. The wardens want it cut out.
q03.epilogue: Green returns to the glade's edge within the season; what soured the heartwood is burned and sung over.

q04.name: Webs in the Canopy
q04.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q04.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Meriele
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Leshanna, age 9)

q05.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q05.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q05.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q06.name: The Ash-Callers' Rebellion
q06.desc: Shamans who traded the clan's fire for their own. The true shaman pays iron for them silenced.
q06.epilogue: The circle's ashes are scattered on running water; the clan's fire burns in one pit again, where it belongs.

q07.name: The Proving Hunt
q07.desc: The clan sets a beast worth a name. Bring back its hide and the clan pays in iron and standing.
q07.epilogue: The hide hangs in the long-tent and the party's name is spoken at the fire -- iron and standing, paid in full.

q08.name: Rival Warband
q08.desc: A rival clan's raiders cut the herd trails. The warchief pays for them driven off or dead.
q08.epilogue: The rival clan sends a peace-gift of salt and knives; the herd trails run open.

q09.name: The Troll Under the Pass
q09.desc: The mountain road pays tribute in carts and drivers. The clans want the pass opened.
q09.epilogue: Carts roll the pass by the next moon; drivers still spit at the cave mouth for luck.

q10.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q10.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q10.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q11.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q11.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q11.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q12.name: The Engine Gone Feral
q12.desc: They built it too big and fed it too well, and now the lower works belong to it.
q12.epilogue: The lower works are re-lit gallery by gallery; the bosses are already arguing about building it again, bigger.

q13.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q13.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q13.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q14.name: Wolves at the Folds
q14.desc: Sheep gone, then a shepherd. The commons want the pack dug out of the hills.
q14.epilogue: Lambing season passes without a beast lost; the elder salts the den's earth as the old rites ask.

q15.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q15.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q15.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Tomas, a human outlaw, level 5, out there somewhere)

q16.name: The Restless Crypt
q16.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q16.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q17.name: The Hedge-Wizards' Toll
q17.desc: Renegade hedge-wizards tax the road in fire and frost. The witchfinder pays for the tollhouse dark.
q17.epilogue: Carters use the road again by week's end; the witchfinder burns the confiscated grimoires in the market square, twice, for the crowd.

q18.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q18.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q18.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q19.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q19.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q19.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q20.name: The Coven in the Mists
q20.desc: Singers who left the circle and took the old songs with them. The mists over the hollow are not weather.
q20.epilogue: The mists lift over the hollow; the censor seals the stolen songs back into the circle's keeping, and does not say thank you.

q21.name: Webs in the Canopy
q21.desc: The high walkways are silked over and the wardens who went up have not come down.
q21.epilogue: The silk is cut away and the walkways rehung; the lost wardens are carried down for their rites.

giver.name: Mindartis
giver.role: the master of the high walkways

q22.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q22.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q23.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q23.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q23.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q24.name: Wardens Gone Rogue
q24.desc: A warden-band that answers to no council now, taxing the forest roads at arrowpoint.
q24.epilogue: The rogue lodge is unroofed and left to the moss; the forest roads charge no toll but weather.

q25.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q25.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q25.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q26.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q26.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q26.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q27.name: Press-Gang Trouble
q27.desc: A rival boss's press-gang is stealing crews off the night shift. The foreman pays for it stopped, no questions on the how.
q27.epilogue: The night crews walk home unescorted; the rival boss's tower stands quiet -- under new management within the week.

q28.name: Beasts Maddened by the Blight
q28.desc: Boar and bear alike gone wrong-eyed, tearing through the outlying groves.
q28.epilogue: The groves go quiet again; the keeper plants a sapling for every beast that had to die.

q29.name: The Restless Crypt
q29.desc: The churchyard dead are not staying put. The parish purse opens for whoever settles them.
q29.epilogue: The graves are re-consecrated at dusk; the parish sleeps its first whole night in weeks.

q30.name: Wyvern on the Crags
q30.desc: A shadow over the herds, and herders who point at the high crags and will not climb.
q30.epilogue: The herders climb again; the skull goes up on a spear above the trail, warning and boast at once.

q31.name: Deserters Turned Raiders
q31.desc: Soldiers who kept their steel and lost their oaths, living off the villages they once guarded.
q31.epilogue: The recovered blades go back to the armory; the villages post no watch tonight.

q32.name: Bandits on the King's Road
q32.desc: Wagons stripped, a toll of blood at the fords. The reeve pays for the road made safe.
q32.epilogue: The fords run quiet again; carters raise a cup to the party at every wayhouse on the road.

q33.name: The Boiler-Cult
q33.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q33.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

traits.quirk: has a child (with them: Zonk, age 11)

q42.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q42.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q43.name: The Renegade Magus
q43.desc: A court wizard who stopped asking leave. The crown pays for the tower dark and the fires out.
q43.epilogue: The tower stands dark and cold; the crown's sage carries the renegade's books away under seal.

q44.name: Spiders in the Undercity
q44.desc: The vent shafts are webbed shut and the air below is going bad. Small pay, small heroes welcome.
q44.epilogue: Air moves in the undercity again; small pay, but the vent-warden's word is now good in every burrow.

q45.name: Scrap-Hounds Loose in the Works
q45.desc: The bosses' hound-pack slipped its chains and dens in the gearworks. Pay docked for every eaten shift-worker.
q45.epilogue: The works run a full shift without a scream; the foreman docks nobody, this once.

q46.name: The Boiler-Cult
q46.desc: A cult that worships the works' oldest boiler and feeds it what it asks for. It has started asking for shift-workers.
q46.epilogue: The old boiler is decommissioned with full honors and sold for scrap; the inspector stamps the paperwork DONE with visible pleasure.

q47.name: The Prisoner's Ransom
q47.desc: A magistrate buys a hostage back from across the border. The gold must arrive uncounted and unopened.
q47.epilogue: The hostage walks free at the next new moon; the broker, professionally offended by honesty, pays out to the coin.

q48.name: Medicine for the Outbreak
q48.desc: A sickness across the border, and the cure sitting here in a stockroom. Every day on the road is counted in graves.
q48.epilogue: The crate is empty within two days and the burial details stand down; the warden keeps the party's names for the record of the outbreak.

w1.name: Strange Salvage
w1.desc: Livestock, fence-iron, and one census-taker have gone missing along the human border. What the trackers found was half animal, half machine, and all appetite.
w1.epilogue: The burrow is collapsed onto its own bomb stores; the crater smokes for a week and the livestock stop vanishing.

traits.quirk: has an enemy (Bolg, a orc disgraced soldier, level 3, out there somewhere)
